title: Integrating AI in the Classroom – Delaware Department of Education

State and local policymakers and academic leaders should establish an equitable framework supporting comprehensive policies catering to all students' diverse needs and capabilities. title: AI in Delaware Education – Delaware Department of Education
snippet: AI in Delaware Education - Delaware Department of Education Skip to content Agencies News Topics Contact Search AI in Delaware Education ddoe_admin 2026-03-19T11:02:48-04:00 Digital DE High-Quality Instructional Materials Instructional Resources Professional Learning Supporting Flexible Learning Assessment Supporting the Whole Child Family Resources Summer Acceleration Searchable Index Digital DE › Instructional Resources › AI in Delaware Education AI in Delaware Education Guidance that support districts and charters in understanding and implementing instructional AI tools Integrating Generative Artificial Intelligence (Gen AI) in K-12 education represents a transformative opportunity to enhance teaching and learning. By leveraging the power of these technologies, educators can foster creativity, streamline administrative tasks, and provide personalized learning experiences for students. However, this shift also requires thoughtful management to ensure responsible, ethical, and equitable use while protecting the privacy of all learners. This guidance aligns with the four key policy areas outlined in Delaware's Generative AI in the K-12 Education Guidance: Protecting s

notes: Georgia Department of Education AI in K-12 guidance landing page.
snippet: Leveraging AI in the K-12 Setting - GaDOE Community About User Agreement Privacy Policy FAQ Grant Opportunities Guidance & Policy Professional Learning Newsletters About User Agreement Privacy Policy FAQ Grant Opportunities Guidance & Policy Professional Learning Newsletters © 2026 Georgia Department of Education Leveraging AI in the K-12 Setting GaDOE’s guidance outlines general information and best practices to ensure the ethical, effective, and secure use of AI tools and systems within our schools. Through continued collaboration, training, and adherence to these guidelines, Georgia schools can harness the power of AI to create a more personalized, efficient, and innovative educational environment for all students. Date Published: Jan 21, 2025 Intended Audience: District Leaders, School Leaders, Technology Primary Contact : Policy Division DOE Office: Policy, Flexibility & External Affairs Topics: Policy/Legal, Technology Document Type: Guidance Government Level: State Document URL: https://url.gadoe.org/bl775 © 2026 Georgia Department of Education
